title: GDSII loader test corpus
description: Curated catalog of GDSII (.gds/.gds2) files discovered in public GitHub repositories by the pastebom.com-tester discovery agent. Each case is pinned to an immutable commit SHA so it can be downloaded reproducibly and fed to the pcb-extract GDSII loader. Intended to drive a download-and-parse test that emits a per-case success/failure report.

selection.method: group by blob SHA; one case per unique file, every location kept as a source pointer (no per-repo cap)

tiers.smoke: <= 64 KiB — tiny cells/gates; fast default CI subset

fields.id: stable slug, unique within this file; use as the test name
fields.name: short human label
fields.blob_sha: git blob SHA = content hash; the case identity. Verify downloads against it.
fields.date_indexed: date this catalog entry was built (not original discovery date; per-file discovery timestamps were not retained)
fields.source_count: number of byte-identical locations this file was found at
fields.sources: list of every location holding this exact file: {repo, path, permalink, raw_url, commit, commit_date, repo_stars, repo_license}. Any one can be downloaded; multiple act as fallback mirrors.
fields.repo / path / permalink / raw_url / comm: convenience copy of the PRIMARY source (the most-starred location); identical to sources[0].
fields.expect: OPTIONAL, absent by default. Add "ok" or "fail" to turn a case from report-only into an assertion once expected behavior is known.

usage: A test downloads raw_url (or any of `sources`) for each case (optionally filtered by tier), runs the GDSII loader, and records pass/fail + error. Verify each download against blob_sha for integrity; if the primary raw_url 404s, fall back to another entry in `sources`. With no `expect` field a case is report-only; set `expect` to assert. Default CI should run tier==smoke; run standard/stress behind an opt-in flag (network + size).
